title: Executive Order 12208—Consultations on the Admission of Refugees

flags.justification: Procedural and consultative functions are delegated to State, Justice, and the Coordinator for Refugee Affairs, while the most consequential decisions (specifying special circumstances, key determinations, fixing refugee numbers) are explicitly reserved to the President himself (Sec. 1-103).
A required, statute-anchored consultation process with the Judiciary Committees of both chambers of Congress.
Multiple related functions coherently implement one statute, the Refugee Act of 1980.
A delegation-of-functions order implementing an existing statute, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing implementation of a permanent statute.
The order's entire purpose is to implement congressionally-mandated consultation requirements with the Judiciary Committees, preserving rather than removing congressional oversight.
No third-party compliance regime is created by this delegation order.
Implements the Refugee Act of 1980, the landmark, still-operative statute establishing the modern U.S. refugee admissions framework, enacted in direct response to the Indochinese refugee crisis.
A careful, statute-anchored division of roles among State, Justice, and the Coordinator for Refugee Affairs.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 1/12 = 8.3%. Implements the Refugee Act of 1980, still the foundational framework for U.S. refugee admissions decades later, yet scores low due to the careful reservation of the most consequential decisions to the President and the preserved congressional-consultation requirement.
